latin: Harrius solus in conclave subterraneum descendit et Professorem Quirrell ibi invenit, non Snape ut suspicatus erat. Quirrell tegumentum capitis removit et in occipite eius vultum Voldemortis revelavit — duo corpora unam vitam dividebant. Voldemort Lapidem Philosophi desiderabat ut corpus proprium recuperaret. ante Speculum Erisedis Harrius stetit: speculum ei Lapidem in sinu ostendit, quod solum is qui Lapidem invenire sed non uti vellet eum accipere posset. cum Quirrell Harrium tangeret, cutis eius ardebat — amor matris Harrium protegebat. Quirrell in cinerem cecidit et Voldemort ut umbra fugit.

questions.prompt: Who did Harry find in the underground chamber?

questions.target: On the back of Quirrell's head
choices: On the back of Quirrell's head
In the shadows of the room

questions.prompt: How did Harry obtain the Philosopher's Stone from the Mirror?
questions.target: Only someone who wanted to find it but not use it could receive it
choices: Only someone who wanted to find it but not use it could receive it
Dumbledore gave it to him through the mirror

questions.prompt: Why did Quirrell burn when he touched Harry?

translation: Harry descended alone into the underground chamber and found Professor Quirrell there, not Snape as he had suspected. Quirrell removed his turban and revealed Voldemort's face on the back of his head — two bodies shared one life. Voldemort desired the Philosopher's Stone to recover his own body. Harry stood before the Mirror of Erised: the mirror showed him the Stone in his pocket, because only one who wanted to find the Stone but not use it could receive it. When Quirrell touched Harry, his skin burned — his mother's love protected Harry. Quirrell fell to ash and Voldemort fled as a shadow.
